current_title: Head of Production Engineering, BMW Group EMEA

career_history.title: Head of Production Engineering, BMW Group EMEA
career_history.scope: Led 310 engineers and specialists with €96M annual capex envelope for process engineering standards, line design and industrialization support across multiple plants.

key_achievements.achievement: Reduced average commissioning overrun on major line projects from 11.2 weeks to 4.6 weeks by introducing modular equipment approval gates.
Standardized digital line-simulation templates across 5 plants, reducing engineering change requests after design freeze from 184 to 109.
Improved capex forecast accuracy from 82.1% to 94.5% on industrialization portfolio.

career_history.title: Director, EV Industrialization Programs
career_history.scope: Directed 140 engineers and program managers across EV launch engineering workstreams with €72M budget responsibility.

key_achievements.achievement: Closed 91 of 103 red-rated industrialization risks before SOP on two electrified vehicle programs.
Shortened tooling release cycle from 17 weeks to 12 weeks through parallel validation logic.

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Promoted to enterprise engineering leadership role.

career_history.title: Senior Manager, Automation & Controls Engineering
career_history.scope: Led 65 automation engineers and external integrators with €28M annual project budget for line controls and automation systems.

key_achievements.achievement: Reduced robot commissioning defects from 7.4 per cell to 2.6 per cell on a major body shop upgrade.
Cut PLC-related downtime from 3.1% to 1.4% across target areas.

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Selected for EV industrialization leadership track.

career_history.title: Controls Engineering Lead
career_history.scope: Led automation project teams of 18-25 engineers for industrial motion and controls projects in automotive manufacturing.

key_achievements.achievement: Delivered 14 major controls deployments with average commissioning variance below 3.2%.
Reduced recurring software fault tickets by 37.5% through reusable code libraries.

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Recruited by BMW for automation leadership role.

leadership_indicators.leadership_style_self_described: High-standard technical leader who believes clarity of thinking and engineering rigor are the foundation of production excellence.
leadership_indicators.decision_making_style: Analytical, hypothesis-driven and often faster on technical decisions than on people trade-offs.
leadership_indicators.conflict_handling: Confronts poor logic directly; can sound dismissive when others are not equally prepared.
leadership_indicators.change_orientation: High on technical change, mixed on people-led cultural change.
leadership_indicators.risk_appetite: Moderate; willing to take technical bets when data supports them.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: Repeated late engineering changes were jeopardizing EV launch milestones.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Redesigned design-freeze governance and forced early simulation sign-off discipline across engineering functions.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Post-freeze engineering changes fell from 184 to 109.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: Capex overruns on automation packages created tension with finance and plant leaders.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Introduced standardized investment logic with stage-gate approval and forecast reconciliation cadence.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Forecast accuracy improved from 82.1% to 94.5% within one planning cycle.

performance_reviews.summary: Technically exceptional and highly credible on industrialization complexity; people leadership remains uneven.
development_areas: Needs stronger empathy in leadership interactions
performance_reviews.manager_comments: Christine is among the strongest technical minds in the organization. Her ceiling depends on whether she can lead humans as well as she leads systems.

performance_reviews.summary: Created significant engineering efficiency gains and better capex control.
development_areas: Needs more visible coaching of leaders
performance_reviews.manager_comments: She is promotable on technical merit, but not yet on all-around executive maturity.

performance_reviews.summary: Highly valuable architect of production systems but still not a natural people-first operator.
development_areas: Tendency to win arguments and lose buy-in
performance_reviews.manager_comments: For a technical COO-style role she would be very compelling. For this role, the people equation is a serious concern.

peers: Christine is brilliant. She often sees the root cause before the rest of us finish the problem statement.
Working with her can be difficult when she has low tolerance for imperfect thinking.
She raises the technical bar, but not everyone wants to run through walls for her.
direct_reports: You learn a huge amount from her if you can keep up.
Feedback is sharp and specific, but it can feel cutting rather than developmental.
She protects technical excellence, yet team morale dips when pressure spikes.
senior_leadership: Probably the strongest technical internal candidate.
The gap is not competence; it is whether people would truly follow her in a labor-intensive transformation.

interview_notes.overall_impression: Exceptionally sharp and the most technically sophisticated internal contender. Serious concern on large-scale people leadership and frontline followership.

question_responses.question: How would you handle a 6-month semiconductor supply disruption across 3 plants?
question_responses.response_summary: She would immediately map component criticality to architecture and software dependencies, redesign build combinations to protect throughput, and create a digital decision layer linking engineering, purchasing and production scheduling. She focused less on the human side and more on technical product-configuration optimization.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Very strong systems answer, somewhat underweighted labor and customer communication dimensions.

question_responses.question: How would you accelerate Neue Klasse SOP by 6 months?
question_responses.response_summary: She argued the only credible path is to attack engineering change latency, compress validation loops with digital twins, lock design interfaces earlier and assign a single industrialization owner per critical module. She was confident and specific on where time is really lost in launch programs.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Probably the best technical answer in the internal pool.

question_responses.question: What would you do if Tesla poached 15% of your production engineers?
question_responses.response_summary: She would identify capability bottlenecks, create technical fellow pathways, ringfence critical launch talent and redesign knowledge capture to reduce single-point dependency. She talked more about capability architecture than emotional retention.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Intelligent but colder than ideal for a talent-retention scenario.

interview_notes.case_study_performance: Scored 8.5/10. Elite technical architecture; weaker on the leadership psychology of execution.

reference_checks.quote: Christine is outstanding technically and can solve problems most people cannot even frame. My hesitation is simple: this role is not won on engineering logic alone.
When she is aligned with you, progress is fast. When she thinks you are not prepared, she can run over people.

red_flags: Weak people leadership relative to role complexity and workforce scale.
Limited direct union and frontline production leadership exposure.
Can damage buy-in through overly sharp interpersonal style.
green_flags: Technically brilliant with strong EV industrialization credibility.
Excellent digital manufacturing and engineering governance capability.
Strong capex and launch-risk management track record.
